chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.187 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs For hearing of bail application ------------------------------------------ 03.03.2026 Mr. Ahmed Hussain Jokhio, advocate for applicant Mr. Sharafuddin Kanhar, A.P.G. & Ms. Rukhsana Qassim Mirjat, ADPP M/s Ahmed Ali & Ali Qambar Askari, advocates for complainant ------------------------------------ Applicants Jameel Mendhro, Jamshed Mendhro, Umar and Ali Muhammad alias Ali seek pre-arrest bail in FIR No.233/2025, registered at P.S. Dhabeji, District Thatta for offence under Sections 324, 337-F(i), 504, 34 PPC, after rejection of their bail plea by learned Additional Sessions Judge-I/M.C.T.C., Thatta vide order dated 16.01.2026. At the very outset, learned counsel for applicants submits that the alleged offence is compoundable, the parties have settled their dispute outside the Court and they will file proper application before the concerned Court. However, he submits that complainant has furnished affidavit of no objection. Mr. Ahmed Ali, advocate, files Vakalatnama on behalf of complainant along with affidavit of no objection on pre-arrest bail to the applicants. He submits that the parties have settled their dispute outside the Court therefore the complainant has recorded no objection. Learned A.P.G. has recorded no objection for grant of bail. Heard learned counsel for applicants, learned A.P.G., learned counsel for complainant, and perused the material available on record. The alleged offence is compoundable; all the Sections applied in the FIR are bailable except Section 324, PPC.

Heard learned counsel for applicants, learned A.P.G., learned counsel for complainant, and perused the material available on record. The alleged offence is compoundable; all the Sections applied in the FIR are bailable except Section 324, PPC. During pendency of instant criminal bail application, the parties have settled their dispute outside the Court and the complainant has recorded no objection for confirmation of their pre-arrest bail. In view of above, instant criminal bail application is allowed and the interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicants/accused is confirmed on the same terms and conditions. However, they are directed to file proper application before the concerned Court, in accordance with law. Instant criminal bail application is disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
